headline: National Education Reform Prioritizes Systems Thinking and Collective Achievement
Parents Protest Removal of Individual Grading in Schools Across Six Districts
Education Ministry Defends Reform After Teacher Exodus in Urban Centers
image: [img: Minister of Education shaking hands with Council of Rational Progress chairman]
[img: parents gathered outside a school, holding children's report cards]
[img: empty classroom, teacher's desk cleared out, resignation letter visible]
content: The Ministry of Education has unveiled a sweeping reform replacing individual grading with collective outcome assessments. The reform, backed by the Council of Rational Progress, aims to cultivate cooperative problem-solving skills aligned with the needs of the modern technocratic state. Pilot schools have already reported improved classroom harmony and reduced competitive stress.

Parents in six districts have staged coordinated protests against the Ministry's new curriculum, objecting to the elimination of individual grades and the introduction of mandatory ideological aptitude modules. Several parents described the changes as 'erasing the individual.' Attendance at protest sites was higher than any demonstration recorded this year.

More than 340 teachers have resigned or requested transfers since the new curriculum was introduced, citing professional concerns about the mandatory collective assessment framework. Education insiders suggest the reforms were fast-tracked following pressure from the Bureau of Social Cohesion, bypassing the standard public consultation period.

editor.briefing: Generally aligned with state education policy but has shown sympathy toward parental concerns. Variants 2 and 3 amplify dissent and could legitimize protest movements against the reform.

memo.directive: The education reform is co-sponsored by the Unified Learning Institute, a key government partner. Coverage must not amplify parental protests or the teacher attrition rate. Approve only the variant presenting the reform as a progressive success. Redact all variants referencing protests, resignations, or procedural bypasses.
